Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code). Session `1b7a3403-06f3-4862-be80-479e129656de`.

FAIR-band: in-band [10/30 — current author count over last 30 merged]

Refs #11584 (delivers AC1 / Lane A only; sibling lanes still pending). #11584 stays open until Lane C merges (PR [#11586](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11586)) and Lane B disposition (ADR 0011 amendment) lands per operator decision. Lane B was deferred per @neo-gpt's framing in `MESSAGE:d2bdffaa` and noted below.

**Close-target discipline:** intentionally non-magic `Refs` keyword over the magic-resolver keyword family so squash-merge does not auto-finalize the parent ticket with residual Lane B still pending. Cycle-1 hazard avoided proactively (companion PR #11586 caught the same drift via @neo-gpt review `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAQ3whQ` + `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAQ7ASA` Cycle 2 on this PR).

## Summary

Pure `git revert 3d4c01f7` of PR [#11571](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11571) (`docs(agentos): add semantic anchors to agent maps (#11561)`). Restores the three Map / Atlas substrate files to their pre-#11571 byte counts per Discussion #11577 graduation ([`DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/discussions/11577#discussioncomment-16965519)) and operator forward-rule (rejection of any new `<a>` anchor-tag insertion in substrate PRs).

## Damage Removed

| File | Pre-revert (post-#11571) | Post-revert | Delta | `<a id>` tags removed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `AGENTS.md` (Map) | 22,649 | **21,106** | -1,543 | 13 |
| `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` (Map, referrer-only) | 24,092 | **23,683** | -409 | 0 |
| `learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md` (Atlas) | 20,815 | **18,409** | -2,406 | 23 |
| **Total** | **67,556** | **63,198** | **-4,358** | **36** |

`AGENTS.md` headroom restored from 1,927 bytes to **3,470 bytes** under the 24,576-byte `check-substrate-size.mjs` mechanical cap. Future map-substrate work regains its previously-available instruction budget.

## Mechanic: pure `git revert` over in-place strip

@neo-gpt suggested in-place strip in `MESSAGE:d2bdffaa` (remove `<a id>` tags + md-link payloads, preserve other heading changes). I chose pure `git revert 3d4c01f7` instead for three reasons:

1. **Mechanical zero-error guarantee** — operator framing "no room for more mistakes." Pure revert is a single git operation with no author-judgment surface; in-place strip would require per-file decisions about which heading additions to preserve and which deletions (the old positional `§N` refs) to restore manually.
2. **#11571 diff substance** — 96 insertions / 50 deletions = 146 changes across 3 files. The deletions are old positional `§N` references that the operator-corrected substrate explicitly PERMITS (Discussion #11577 graduation). Pure revert restores them exactly to their pre-#11571 shape; in-place strip risks losing the exact prior content.
3. **Review surface** — pure revert is a single-commit PR with diff exactly mirroring #11571's. Trivial cross-family V-B-A via `git show HEAD` against `git show 3d4c01f7`.

## V-B-A: Revert Feasibility

- **Zero post-#11571 commits touched the 3 substrate files** (verified `git log 3d4c01f7..origin/dev -- AGENTS.md AGENTS_STARTUP.md learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md` returns empty before this PR).
- **Revert applied cleanly** — no merge conflicts; `git revert --no-edit -m 1 3d4c01f7680ecc1258c798af1587f99b7fc7fd54` succeeded on first attempt.
- **Empirical byte restoration verified**: `wc -c` on all 3 files matches pre-#11571 baseline exactly (21,106 / 23,683 / 18,409).
- **`<a id>` tag count verified zero** via `grep -cE '<a[[:space:]]+id="'` on all 3 files post-revert.

## Architectural Impact

- **Map vs World Atlas compaction discipline (ADR 0007) restored.** Multi-week operator-led substrate evolution is no longer competing with anchor-tag scaffolding budget.
- **Discussion #11577 graduation operationalized.** The graduated form (`§<ref>` text-only stable identifier; no `<a id>` anchor tag scaffolding) is now in effect on `dev`.
- **Cross-family forward-rule enforcement substantiated.** GPT (`MESSAGE:a9aee65c`) + me committed to mirror `<a>` tag review-blocker discipline. PR #11586 (Lane C lint inversion) mechanically enforces the rule on future PRs.

## Edge Cases

- **`§N` positional refs restored** in AGENTS.md / AGENTS_STARTUP.md / AGENTS_ATLAS.md. Operator-corrected substrate explicitly permits both positional `§\d+` and semantic `§<kebab>` forms; the prior lint that blocked `§\d+` in skill files is inverted under Lane C / PR #11586.
- **No impact on `.agents/skills/**`** — this PR touches only the 3 Map/Atlas substrate files. Skill substrate remains as-is.
- **ADR 0011 still encodes the wrong worked example** — Lane B (ADR amendment) is noted as residual technical debt below; not blocking on this revert. Future agents authoring substrate-numbering work should consult Discussion #11577 graduation, not ADR 0011 §2.1 worked example as currently written.

## Test Evidence

- `node ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs --base HEAD` → OK (script's current form on `dev` predates the Lane C inversion; would also OK against the post-Lane-C form since no new anchor tags are added by this PR).
- `node buildScripts/util/check-whitespace.mjs` → passed (pre-commit hook).
- `git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD` → passed.
- Byte / anchor-count verification (above) — empirical confirmation that revert restored exact pre-#11571 state.

Evidence: L2 (mechanical revert + empirical byte/tag verification + git history V-B-A) → L2 required (substrate restore is verifiable via static contract). No residuals.

## Residual: Lane B (ADR 0011 amendment)

Per @neo-gpt's framing (`MESSAGE:d2bdffaa`): "leave lint inversion / ADR 0011 supersedure as separate lanes." ADR 0011 §2.1 still encodes `[Text](path.md#anchor-id)` markdown-link form as the worked example — superseded by Discussion #11577 graduation but not yet amended in-source.

**Disposition options for Lane B:**
- (a) Amend ADR 0011 §2.1 / §2.2 in a focused follow-up PR (addresses AC2)
- (b) Supersede ADR 0011 entirely via a new ADR 0012 capturing the corrected substrate
- (c) Leave as paid debt; future agents must consult Discussion #11577 graduation in addition to ADR 0011

This is filed as known residual; operator-decision on shape. Neither blocking nor urgent given that:
- The lint substrate (PR #11586) enforces the corrected form at CI time
- The graduation comment (`DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P`) provides the durable substrate-correctness reference
- No active PR is implementing the (now-superseded) ADR 0011 worked example

## Cross-Family Review Mandate

Per `pull-request-workflow.md §6.1`. Requesting **@neo-gpt** as primary reviewer once #11585 (gh sync 504 retry) lane has bandwidth. A2A handoff follows.

Requested action: use `/pr-review` on this PR (mechanic verification + byte count V-B-A is trivial).
